call_transcript:
Agent: Hi Eleanor, this is Mark calling from Finance Solutions. I see you recently inquired about a personal loan on our website. Um, how are you doing today? 
Eleanor: Oh, hi. Yeah, I was just looking at options. 
Agent: Great! So, we're pre-approving you for a $10,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 8.99%. That's, uh, based on the information you provided. What are you hoping to use the funds for, if you don't mind me asking? 
Eleanor: I'm planning a kitchen renovation. It's… it's a bit of a project. 
Agent: A kitchen renovation, fantastic! That'll definitely add value to your home. Now, with this loan, you'd have fixed monthly payments over, say, a five-year term. We can adjust that, of course. The total cost, including interest, would be… let me just calculate… around $19,800. Does that sound manageable? 
Eleanor: Hmm, that's… okay. What are the fees associated with the loan? 
Agent: There's a small origination fee of 1%, so $100, and then standard late payment fees if applicable. But honestly, we try to work with our customers. 
Eleanor: Okay. And is that the best rate you can offer? I've been looking at a couple of other places. 
Agent: That is a very competitive rate, Eleanor, especially given your excellent credit score. We can lock that in for you today. We can also look at a slightly shorter term to reduce the overall interest paid. 
Eleanor: Alright, let's do it. Lock it in.

Agent: Hello, Mr. Miller? This is Sarah calling from Finance Solutions. We're offering a special promotion on our Signature Credit Card. 
Robert: Where did you get my number? I didn't give anyone permission to call me. 
Agent: Sir, we acquired your information through… uh… publicly available sources. We're pre-approving you for a $2500 Signature Credit Card with an interest rate of 19.99%. 
Robert: 19.99?! Are you kidding me? That's outrageous! I already have a credit card. 
Agent: Well, sir, this card offers rewards points and… 
Robert: No, thank you. I'm not interested. And please, take me off your list. *click*

Agent: Hi Jessica, this is David from Finance Solutions. I'm calling regarding a referral from GreenTech Solutions. They mentioned you might be interested in a personal loan. 
Jessica: Oh, yeah, Mark from GreenTech said you guys were good. I'm looking at consolidating some debt. 
Agent: Excellent! We can definitely help with that. We're pre-approving you for a $15,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 7.49%. That's a really competitive rate, especially with your credit score. 
Jessica: Okay, that sounds good. What's the catch? 
Agent: No catch! Just standard loan terms. We have a few different repayment options, from three to seven years. Let's say five years… your monthly payment would be around $290. 
Jessica: And what about fees? Are there any hidden fees? 
Agent: There's a one-time origination fee of 0.5%, so $75. And, of course, late payment fees if you happen to miss a payment. But we send reminders, so that's rarely an issue. We also have a prepayment penalty if you pay off the loan early, which is 2% of the remaining balance. 
Jessica: A prepayment penalty? That doesn't sound great. 
Agent: It's standard practice, Jessica. It helps us cover our administrative costs. But we can discuss waiving it if you maintain a good payment history. 
Jessica: Hmm, I'll need to think about that. Can you send me a detailed breakdown of all the fees and terms in an email? 
Agent: Absolutely! I'll send that over right away. And feel free to call me back if you have any questions.

Agent: Hi Michael, this is Lisa calling from Finance Solutions. I see you filled out an application for our Signature Credit Card online. 
Michael: Oh, right. Yeah, I was just checking what my options were. 
Agent: Great! We're pre-approving you for a $5000 Signature Credit Card at an interest rate of 14.99%. It offers cash back rewards on all purchases. 
Michael: 14.99%? That's a little higher than I was hoping for. I was thinking more around 12%. 
Agent: Unfortunately, that's the best rate we can offer you at this time, given your credit history. However, you can always try to improve your credit score and reapply in the future. 
Michael: Hmm, okay. I'll probably just stick with my current card then. Thanks anyway. 
Agent: Alright, Michael. If you change your mind, feel free to give us a call. Have a good day.

Agent: Hello, Ms. Rodriguez? This is Sarah calling from Finance Solutions. 
Patricia: (Yelling) You guys called me *yesterday*! I told you I'm not interested! Where did you even get my number?! 
Agent: Ma'am, I apologize for the inconvenience. We're offering a $3000 Personal Loan at a rate of 24.99%… 
Patricia: 24.99?! Are you serious? I can't afford that! And I want you to remove my number from your list *immediately*! This is harassment! 
Agent: Ma'am, I understand your frustration… 
Patricia: No, you don't! Just take me off the list! *click*
